ocr.text: The analysis presents data segmented by age group regarding the correlation of EV with age and IOD. For Children (n = 515),
the correlation for OS is 0.114 with a p-value of 0.010, and for IOD, it is 0.607 with a p-value of <0.001; for OD, the correlation
remains 0.114 with a p-value of 0.010, while for IOD, it is 0.606 with a p-value of <0.001. Teenagers (n = 517) show a
correlation of 0.207 for OS with a p-value of <0.001 and 0.365 for IOD with a p-value of <0.001; for OD, the correlation is
0.217 with a p-value of <0.001 and 0.372 for IOD with a p-value of <0.001. Adults (n = 582) exhibit a correlation of 0.038 for
OS with a p-value of 0.366 and 0.318 for IOD with a p-value of <0.001; for OD, the correlation is 0.027 with a p-value of 0.511
and 0.323 for IOD with a p-value of <0.001.
Age Group (no.)  Correlation of EV with Age  Correlation of EV with IOD
R  P  R  P
Elderly (n = 396) show a correlation of −0.207 for OS with a p-value of <0.001 and 0.308 for IOD with a p-value of <0.001; for
OD, the correlation is −0.199 with a p-value of <0.001 and 0.324 for IOD with a p-value of <0.001. The Total (n = 2010)
presents a correlation of 0.230 for OS with a p-value of <0.001 and 0.537 for IOD with a p-value of <0.001; for OD, the
correlation is 0.223 with a p-value of <0.001 and 0.536 for IOD with a p-value of <0.001.
